The post

title: Canadian Lumber Duties Expected to Drop This Summer
summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce has signaled that it plans to lower antidumping and countervailing tariffs later this year on imports of Canadian softwood lumber products from the current rate of about 35.16% to 24.83% following its annual review of existing tariffs.

title: 3 Key Reasons to Become a Speaker at IBS 2027
summary: What if one speaking opportunity could elevate your reputation, expand your network and put your ideas in front of thousands of the people shaping the future of home building? That’s exactly what the NAHB International Builders’ Show® (IBS) offers.

title: White House Economic Report Cites NAHB Regulatory Housing Studies
summary: The White House today released its Economic Report of the President (ERP) that contains a section on housing and homeownership that cites NAHB regulatory studies.

title: New York Builders Win Legal Challenge on Onerous Wetlands Rule
summary: In an important win for New York home builders and housing affordability, the New York State Supreme Court has issued a decision annulling the state’s new definition of what a “freshwater wetland” is based on violations of the State Environmental Quality Review Act (SEQRA).

title: Podcast: Housing Market Braces for Tense Spring Home Buying Season
summary: In the latest episode of NAHB’s podcast, Housing Developments, CEO Jim Tobin and COO Paul Lopez delve into market uncertainties ahead of the spring home buying season, efforts to bolster housing supply, what the 2027 budget cuts could mean for housing and how members can engage in the codes process.

title: Home Remodeling Profit Margin Jumps on Demand and Business Practices
summary: Profitability for residential remodelers reached its highest level in nearly 30 years in 2024, according to NAHB’s most recent Remodelers’ Cost of Doing Business Study.

title: The States and Construction Trades Most Reliant on Immigrant Workers
summary: Immigrant work totaled 26.3% of the entire construction workforce in 2024, a record high. The number is even higher among construction trades.

title: 5 Signals Many Home Builders Are Misreading Right Now
summary: Price cuts. Slower sales. Rising inventory. Elevated rates. Each of them is real. Each can be measured, but each can be easily misunderstood. For builders, it's critical how they interpret what’s happening and how they respond.

title: Remodeling Market Sentiment Edges Down but Remains Positive in First Quarter
summary: The National Association of Home Builders (NAHB) released its NAHB/Westlake Royal Remodeling Market Index (RMI) for the first quarter, posting a reading of 62. While this reading is down two points from the previous quarter, it is still solidly in positive territory.

title: Watch Livestream of Virtual Spring Board of Directors Meeting
summary: The NAHB Board of Directors will convene virtually on Tuesday, April 14, at 10 a.m. ET. A livestream is available on nahb.org for NAHB members and HBA executive officers who would like to observe the meeting.

title: Tuition-Free Alabama Trades Academy Backed by Key Community Support
summary: As part of a statewide effort to expand trades education, the Home Builders Association of Metro Mobile (HBAMM) launched the South Alabama Homebuilding Academy (SAHA), an eight-week, tuition-free program to prepare adults for careers in residential construction.

title: ICC Public Comment Hearings on Proposed Building Code Changes Begin April 19
summary: The International Code Council (ICC) will hold its combined Public Comment Hearings for the 2024-2027 code cycle beginning April 19 in Hartford, Conn. NAHB members interested in building codes are encouraged to attend or watch a livestream of the hearings.

title: Trump Seeks Nearly $11 Billion Cut to HUD Programs
summary: President Trump has proposed a budget that would cut non-defense discretionary spending by $73 billion for fiscal year 2027, which runs from Oct. 1, 2026, through Sept. 30, 2027. The spending reductions include a $10.7 billion cut — about 13% — for the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD).

title: Emerging Green Building Professional Spotlights Innovative Takeaways from IBS
summary: The NAHB IBS Sustainability and Green Building Scholarship aims to provide emerging green builders exposure to the world of high-performance homes and help them jump-start their professional journey by attending the International Builders’ Show (IBS). This year’s winner is Grace Weger, a green builder making a meaningful impact in the world of affordable housing.

title: Construction Helps Lead Job Growth in March
summary: The U.S. labor market showed signs of a modest rebound in March following a weak February, as payroll employment increased and the unemployment rate edged down to 4.3%. Job growth was led by construction, healthcare, and transportation and warehousing.

title: NAHB Chairman Lays Out Vision for Future of the Federation in New Video
summary: NAHB Chairman Bill Owens has launched Blueprint to 100, a modernization initiative in anticipation of NAHB’s 100th anniversary in 2042.

title: NAHB’s Monthly Update Features a Codes Victory and Economic Snapshot
summary: The talking points this month feature news related to federal energy code mandates and the current economic conditions for the housing industry.

title: Call Before You Dig: 6 Key Steps to Prevent Utility Strikes on the Jobsite
summary: April’s National Safe Digging Month is a timely reminder for builders, contractors and trade partners to prioritize one of the most critical and often overlooked jobsite safety practices: preventing utility strikes.

title: NAHB Offers Customizable Materials, Shop Talks for New Homes Month
summary: Every April, NAHB celebrates New Homes Month, a time to spotlight the benefits of new construction to buyers across the country.

title: Register Now for NAHB’s Spring Leadership Meeting and Legislative Conference
summary: NAHB committee and council members, delegates to the Leadership Council, members of the Board of Directors, and executive officers should make plans now to attend the Spring Leadership Meeting and Legislative Conference, June 9-13, at the Grand Hyatt Washington in Washington, D.C.

title: MBA chief economist says mortgage rates, inflation will stay elevated through 2026
summary: MBA’s Mike Fratantoni expects 2026 inflation near 4% and no Fed cuts, keeping mortgage rates and bond yields elevated.

title: California lawmakers weigh state-backed loans for downtown housing towers
summary: Assembly Bill 2074, dubbed the Downtown Revitalization Act, would ease approvals and offer state-backed, low-interest financing near major transit hubs.

title: White House backs deregulation as it looks to address estimated 10M housing deficit
summary: White House ties housing costs to overregulation and a deficit of 10 million single-family homes as builders face high costs and shrinking margins.

title: Brynn Carmody’s burnout as a real estate agent sparked an AI platform for the entire industry
summary: Her Market Lab launched Monday, offering agents a CRM with integrated AI, brand voice tools and compliance document inputs.

title: Why is housing inventory growth slowing down in 2026?
summary: Inventory is up from record lows, but new listings remain below normal, keeping 2026 growth rates below last year’s peak.

title: UWM, Mat Ishbia sued after Michigan State football coach’s harassment fallout
summary: Brenda Tracy, who reported sexual harassment against former Michigan State University head football coach Mel Tucker, is suing United Wholesale Mortgage (UWM) and CEO Mat Ishbia.

title: As policy uncertainty rattles older investors, how can reverse mortgage pros respond?
summary: Better integration of housing wealth through reverse mortgages could help, although reputational and educational hurdles persist.

title: Veterans United seeks dismissal of VA impersonation, steering lawsuit
summary: Veterans United Home Loans filed a motion to dismiss a class-action lawsuit that accuses the lender of falsely presenting itself as affiliated with the federal government and steering borrowers toward more costly loans.

title: Broeksmit outlines next steps as MBA ramps up advocacy efforts
summary: At MBA's National Advocacy Conference, Bob Broeksmit urged members to intensify advocacy as lawmakers consider housing policy changes.

title: As Compass grows, scrutiny builds over private listings and double-ending
summary: CPC review of 5,000 sales finds Compass at 29.7% to 39.5% share in five metros, raising double-ending and private listing concerns.

title: Lane Construction wins $582M Florida interstate job
summary: The Charlotte, North Carolina-based civil contractor will build express lanes on the Moving I-4 Forward program, which is part of the largest infrastructure project in the state.

title: Texas A&M breaks ground on $226M semiconductor R&D facility
summary: The school’s upcoming Semiconductor Institute building is part of the Texas CHIPS Act enacted in 2023 to attract investment to the state and help organizations, colleges and universities develop semiconductor programs.

title: Iran war impacts on oil prices spiked construction stress, increased abandonments
summary: Construction saw a 22.8% surge in project abandonments month over month in March, which ConstructConnect linked to economic impacts brought on by the war.

title: Veteran architect wants more women on jobsites
summary: Gail Sullivan, founder of Boston-based Studio G Architects, said conditions have improved for women in construction, but she still wants to see more progress.

title: Federal appeals court allows White House ballroom construction to resume
summary: A three-judge panel ruled work can continue this week and directed a lower court to consider the national security risks involved in a construction stoppage.

title: Renovation backlog for college facilities hits new highs
summary: The dollar value of deferred capital projects grew by 8% last year, according to the latest state of higher ed facilities report from Gordian.

title: Suffolk tapped as construction manager for $1.2B student housing project
summary: The Boston-based contractor plans to use a unique modular stacking construction method to ensure accuracy and cut down on delivery time.

title: Construction’s economic outlook is increasingly cloudy
summary: Aside from the artificial intelligence buildout, the industry’s numbers have weakened. The boom “probably will end at some point. But not now,” said an economist.

title: Beyond winning work: The key to contractors’ sustained growth
summary: When growth outpaces proper alignment, pitfalls can appear. Learn what to “know before you grow.”

title: Balancing control and efficiency: When to use construction takeoff services
summary: Save time without losing control. Learn when a hybrid approach to material takeoffs is best.
